title: Review RunTime Meters On The VISU_DATA Screen
summary: Use the RunTime Meters section on the VISU_DATA screen to view accumulated operating time values and interpret them as the length of time the item has been in operation since system start. The source also states these values are only reset if the control software is overwritten.
when_to_use: Use when an operator needs to review RunTime Meters on the VISU_DATA screen to understand accumulated operating time since system start, or to record the displayed values for operational tracking.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to reset runtime values.
Do not use this runbook as a control software overwrite procedure.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Access the operator station HMI, navigate to the VISU_DATA screen, read the RunTime Meters values, interpret them using the source definition, and record them if needed.

safety_notes: This source section is informational and does not provide a reset action for RunTime Meters.
Do not attempt to reset runtime values based on this source section.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI

access_required: Access to the operator station HMI
system_preconditions: The operator station HMI is available.
The VISU_DATA screen can be accessed.

external_references.quote_or_summary: The RunTime Meters section provides data on the length of time that the item has been in operation since the start of the system and will only be reset if the control software is overwritten.

visual_references.description: Operator Station HMI VISU_DATA screen layout reference for locating the data screen.
Operator station display controls showing how to navigate to the VISU_DATA screen, including F5 mapping.
Additional VISU_DATA screen reference showing data-screen presentation style.

steps.title: Open the VISU_DATA screen and locate the RunTime Meters section
steps.purpose: Get to the correct HMI screen where the RunTime Meters information is displayed.
steps.instruction: At the operator station HMI, navigate to the VISU_DATA screen and locate the RunTime Meters section.
steps.expected_result: The VISU_DATA screen is open and the RunTime Meters section is visible to the operator.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can access the VISU_DATA screen and identify the area used for runtime information.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot access the VISU_DATA screen or cannot identify the RunTime Meters section from the available screen references.

actions.label: Locate RunTime Meters
actions.exact_action: Locate the RunTime Meters section on the VISU_DATA screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the VISU_DATA screen image as the primary reference for the data-screen layout while locating the RunTime Meters section.
Use the display controls reference for navigation to the VISU_DATA screen, including the F5 mapping to Visu_Data.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the VISU_DATA screen cannot be accessed from the operator station HMI.
Escalate if the runtime values cannot be located on the VISU_DATA screen using the available source references.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The listed control mapping includes F5 to "Visu_Data."
Figure 4-20, Operator Station HMI Data Screen, appears in section 4.1.2.2 VISU_DATA SCREEN.

steps.title: Read the displayed RunTime Meters values
steps.purpose: Capture the runtime values shown for the item or items displayed on the VISU_DATA screen.
steps.instruction: Read the displayed RunTime Meters values for the item or items shown on the VISU_DATA screen.
steps.expected_result: The operator has identified the current runtime values displayed in the RunTime Meters section.
steps.healthy_condition: Runtime values are visible and readable on the VISU_DATA screen.
steps.failure_condition: Runtime values are missing, unreadable, or appear unavailable on the VISU_DATA screen.

actions.label: Read runtime values
actions.exact_action: Read the displayed RunTime Meters values for the item or items shown.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Review the VISU_DATA screen layout while reading the displayed data fields.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if runtime values are not visible or cannot be read.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The RunTime Meters section provides data on the length of time that the item has been in operation since the start of the system and will only be reset if the control software is overwritten.

steps.title: Interpret the RunTime Meters values
steps.purpose: Ensure the displayed values are understood according to the source definition.
steps.instruction: Interpret the displayed RunTime Meters values as the length of time the item has been in operation since the start of the system.
steps.expected_result: The operator understands that the displayed values represent accumulated operating time since system start.
steps.healthy_condition: The values are interpreted as accumulated operation time since the start of the system.
steps.failure_condition: The values are interpreted as something other than accumulated operation time since system start.

actions.label: Interpret runtime meaning
actions.exact_action: Interpret the displayed values as the length of time the item has been in operation since the start of the system.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if runtime values appear inconsistent with expected accumulated operation time.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The RunTime Meters section provides data on the length of time that the item has been in operation since the start of the system and will only be reset if the control software is overwritten.

steps.title: Record the displayed runtime values if needed
steps.purpose: Preserve the observed runtime values for operational tracking when needed.
steps.instruction: Record the displayed runtime values if needed for operational tracking.
steps.expected_result: The runtime values are documented if the operator needs them for tracking.
steps.healthy_condition: The displayed values are captured accurately for later reference.
steps.failure_condition: The values are not recorded when needed, or the recorded values do not match the display.

actions.label: Document runtime values
actions.exact_action: Record the displayed runtime values if needed for operational tracking.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if runtime values appear to have reset unexpectedly during normal operation.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The RunTime Meters section provides data on the length of time that the item has been in operation since the start of the system and will only be reset if the control software is overwritten.

steps.title: Note the reset behavior of RunTime Meters
steps.purpose: Prevent incorrect assumptions about how or when the runtime values reset.
steps.instruction: Note that the source states the RunTime Meters values are only reset if the control software is overwritten.
steps.expected_result: The operator understands the documented reset condition for the RunTime Meters values.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator recognizes that normal use of this screen does not provide a reset procedure and that reset is tied to control software overwrite per the source.
steps.failure_condition: The operator assumes the values can be reset from this screen or observes an unexpected reset during normal operation.

actions.label: Confirm reset behavior
actions.exact_action: Note that the source states these values are only reset if the control software is overwritten.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if runtime values appear to have reset unexpectedly during normal operation.
Stop and escalate if a reset action is being requested based on this source section.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The RunTime Meters section provides data on the length of time that the item has been in operation since the start of the system and will only be reset if the control software is overwritten.

success_criteria: The operator can access the VISU_DATA screen.
The operator can locate and read the RunTime Meters values.
The operator correctly interprets the values as accumulated operating time since system start.
The operator understands that the source states the values are only reset if the control software is overwritten.
healthy_conditions: VISU_DATA is accessible from the operator station HMI.
RunTime Meters values are visible and readable.
Displayed values are understood as accumulated operation time since system start.
No reset action is attempted from this source section.
failure_conditions: The VISU_DATA screen cannot be accessed.
The RunTime Meters section cannot be located or read.
Runtime values appear to have reset unexpectedly during normal operation.
A user attempts to use this source section as a reset procedure.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if runtime values appear to have reset unexpectedly during normal operation.
Escalate if the VISU_DATA screen or RunTime Meters section cannot be accessed or identified.
Do not attempt to reset runtime values based on this source section.
